name: wifi-security-toolkit
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``wifi-security-toolkit`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: sudo python3 <script>.py --iface wlan1 [--output ...]; e.g. `sudo python3 02_recon/04_probe_analyzer.py --iface wlan1 --output room.json`; monitor setup via `sudo python3 01_setup/02_setup_monitor.py start wlan1 --channel 6`; run all from repo root so shared wifi_utils.py resolves.

examples.command: sudo python3 <script>.py --iface wlan1 [--output ...]; e.g. `sudo python3 02_recon/04_probe_analyzer.py --iface wlan1 --output room.json`; monitor setup via `sudo python3 01_setup/02_setup_monitor.py start wlan1 --channel 6`; run all from repo root so shared wifi_utils.py resolves.

dependencies: aircrack-ng suite (monitor mode / iw via system calls)
algorithms: Probe-request PNL extraction per client (preferred SSID list)
OUI vendor fingerprinting via wireshark manuf DB
Hidden-SSID reveal by passive sniff of probe-response/association
Beacon fingerprinting (Wi-Fi gen, WPS, PMF, cipher suites)
KARMA attack detection (AP answering arbitrary directed probes)
GPS wardrive logging to WiGLE-1.4 CSV
Client<->AP association tracking in real time
PMKID extraction from EAPOL M1 (no client needed)
WPS Pixie Dust via reaver wrapper
IE fingerprinting for OS detection surviving MAC randomization
PNL cross-correlation across devices to identify same owner
WIDS sensor (deauth flood, evil twin, probe flood, MAC churn)
Markdown report generation merging JSON outputs
produces: JSON: clients, PNLs, AP maps, handshakes, fingerprints
WiGLE-1.4 CSV (BSSID, SSID, channel, encryption, GPS coords)
Terminal/ASCII real-time visualizations (timeline, frame classifier, probe analyzer)
novelty: Not a thin airodump-ng wrapper — it is pure-Scapy and reimplements frame parsing in wifi_utils.py. Genuinely novel vs the airodump-ng/wash/hcxtools/scapy baseline: (1) PNL cross-correlation across devices to infer common ownership, (2) IE-fingerprint OS identification that survives MAC randomization, (3) KARMA detection (APs responding to any directed probe), (4) RSSI Geiger-counter physical locator, (5) probe-timeline ASCII visualization of device wake/sleep cycles, (6) integrated WIDS for deauth/evil-twin/probe-flood/MAC-churn, (7) WPA3-transition-mode downgrade and PMF-mandate detectors, (8) 802.1X clear-text EAP-identity extraction. However, the bread-and-butter recon (AP scan, client list, hidden-SSID reveal, handshake/PMKID capture, WPS scan, wardrive CSV) substantially overlaps airodump-ng/wash/hcxtools — those modules are redundant with the existing KFIOSA baseline; the distinguishing value is the analysis/correlation/detection layer.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
